title: Spectrum Painting
short description:
A form of SSTV that directly modulates an image to IQ by scanning across it so it shows up when viewed using a waterfall.

Used by Hams for experimentation methods.
description: Spectrum Painting, also known as RF paint, is a form of SSTV that uses an input image directly modulated to IQ over the RF spectrum to produce a raw result, instead of a modulated scanline sequence in the form of an actual analog signal (Robot, Martin, Scottie, PD) or digital bitstream (FSK, PSK, etc).
